name: ETH Session Drive Continuation v1
thesis: ETH 1h is the best-fit primary lane for a session-drive continuation idea only if it is rewritten as dense post-impulse hold logic rather than literal session-window choreography. The edge should come from persistence after a meaningful directional bar that holds value, not from exact clock-based ritual.
invalidation_condition: Abort if post-impulse hold setups remain too sparse, if impulse bars mostly mean-revert immediately, or if continuation cannot cover costs.

pre_submit_reasoning.market_behavior_targeted: A meaningful directional impulse bar shifts short-term flow, and continuation becomes tradable only if the next bar holds above reclaimed value rather than fully retracing.
pre_submit_reasoning.why_edge_should_exist: Even in chop, some intraday expansions create brief persistence. Trading only after the hold should be denser than exact session-timing logic while still respecting the follow-through idea.
pre_submit_reasoning.asset_timeframe_fit: ETH 1h is appropriate because intraday impulse-and-hold structure appears often enough to test density honestly without relying on rare macro bars.
pre_submit_reasoning.likely_failure_mode: If the market stays fully rotational, impulse bars may revert too quickly for a hold-based continuation to persist.
pre_submit_reasoning.first_refinement_path: If participation exists but the runner underperforms, keep the entry and shorten the back-half exit ambition.

density_sanity_check.contradictory_filters: none obvious; impulse, hold, and participation are complementary
density_sanity_check.event_sparsity: managed by broad impulse-and-hold logic instead of exact session windows

long: Close - Open >= 0.28 * ATR_14 OR Close[1] - Open[1] >= 0.28 * ATR_14
short: Open - Close >= 0.28 * ATR_14 OR Open[1] - Close[1] >= 0.28 * ATR_14
